items.name: VoxCPM2

readme.content:
# VoxCPM2

VoxCPM2 is an open-weight multilingual text-to-speech model from OpenBMB. The upstream model card describes it as a tokenizer-free, diffusion-autoregressive 2B parameter TTS model with 48 kHz audio output, natural-language voice design, and reference-audio voice cloning.

LA Studio installs VoxCPM2 through the CrispASR GGUF conversion repository:

- Upstream model: https://huggingface.co/openbmb/VoxCPM2
- GGUF artifacts: https://huggingface.co/cstr/voxcpm2-GGUF
- Runtime: https://github.com/CrispStrobe/CrispASR/releases/tag/v0.8.4

## Supported Languages

Arabic, Burmese, Chinese, Danish, Dutch, English, Finnish, French, German, Greek, Hebrew, Hindi, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Khmer, Korean, Lao, Malay, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Russian, Spanish, Swahili, Swedish, Tagalog, Thai, Turkish, Vietnamese.

## LA Studio Notes

VoxCPM2 requires a CrispASR runtime with the `voxcpm2-tts` backend. LA Studio uses CrispASR v0.8.4 or newer for this model.

Voice design is applied by prepending the voice description to the synthesis text in the upstream VoxCPM2 parenthesized prompt style, for example `(A warm, clear voice)Hello.`. CrispASR's current session API does not expose a separate VoxCPM2 `set_instruct` call; LA Studio encodes the instruction in the text passed to `voxcpm2_synthesize`. Voice cloning uses a reference WAV through CrispASR's unified `crispasr_session_set_voice` API.

items.desc: Tokenizer-free 2B multilingual TTS with 48kHz output, voice design, and zero-shot voice cloning through the CrispASR VoxCPM2 runtime.

items.name: Kokoro 82M (GGUF + Voice Pack)

readme.content:
# Kokoro-82M

Kokoro is an open-weight text-to-speech model published by `hexgrad` on Hugging Face. The upstream card presents it as an 82M-parameter model focused on lightweight, fast, cost-efficient speech generation while still being practical for local and production use.

LA Studio installs Kokoro as a virtual model under `hexgrad/Kokoro-82M`. The downloadable GGUF artifacts are hosted in conversion repositories:

- `cstr/kokoro-82m-GGUF` for the Kokoro backbone GGUF.
- `cstr/kokoro-voices-GGUF` for preset speaker voice packs.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/hexgrad/Kokoro-82M
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/hexgrad/kokoro
- Upstream demo: https://hf.co/spaces/hexgrad/Kokoro-TTS
- Samples: https://huggingface.co/hexgrad/Kokoro-82M/blob/main/SAMPLES.md
- Voices: https://huggingface.co/hexgrad/Kokoro-82M/blob/main/VOICES.md

## Model Facts

- Task: Text-to-Speech
- Parameters: 82M
- License: Apache-2.0
- Current upstream release noted by the model card: v1.0, published 2025-01-27
- Languages and voices noted for v1.0: 8 languages and 54 voices
- Architecture: StyleTTS 2 with an iSTFTNet-derived, decoder-only TTS stack
- Upstream SHA256 for v1.0: `496dba118d1a58f5f3db2efc88dbdc216e0483fc89fe6e47ee1f2c53f18ad1e4`

## Training Notes

The upstream card states that Kokoro was trained on permissive or non-copyrighted audio data with IPA phoneme labels. It lists public-domain audio, permissively licensed audio, and synthetic audio generated by closed-source TTS models as examples of training data sources. The card estimates the total training set at a few hundred hours and reports roughly 1000 A100 80GB GPU-hours across v0.19 and v1.0.

## LA Studio Notes

Kokoro in LA Studio is a preset-voice TTS workflow. It needs one backbone model file and one installed voice pack:

- Default backbone: `kokoro-82m-f16.gguf`
- Default voice pack: `kokoro-voice-af_heart.gguf`

The CrispASR Kokoro runtime depends on `espeak-ng` for phonemizer support. Kokoro voice cloning from reference audio is not enabled in LA Studio; use the packaged voice GGUF files instead.

items.desc: Small, fast multilingual TTS using the CrispASR Kokoro runtime. Requires a Kokoro model GGUF and one kokoro-voice-*.gguf voice pack.

readme.content:
# VoxCPM2

VoxCPM2 is an open-weight multilingual text-to-speech model from OpenBMB. The upstream model card describes it as a tokenizer-free, diffusion-autoregressive 2B parameter TTS model with 48 kHz audio output, natural-language voice design, and reference-audio voice cloning.

LA Studio installs VoxCPM2 through the CrispASR GGUF conversion repository:

- Upstream model: https://huggingface.co/openbmb/VoxCPM2
- GGUF artifacts: https://huggingface.co/cstr/voxcpm2-GGUF
- Runtime: https://github.com/CrispStrobe/CrispASR/releases/tag/v0.8.4

## Supported Languages

Arabic, Burmese, Chinese, Danish, Dutch, English, Finnish, French, German, Greek, Hebrew, Hindi, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Khmer, Korean, Lao, Malay, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Russian, Spanish, Swahili, Swedish, Tagalog, Thai, Turkish, Vietnamese.

## LA Studio Notes

VoxCPM2 requires a CrispASR runtime with the `voxcpm2-tts` backend. LA Studio uses CrispASR v0.8.4 or newer for this model.

Voice design is applied by prepending the voice description to the synthesis text in the upstream VoxCPM2 parenthesized prompt style, for example `(A warm, clear voice)Hello.`. CrispASR's current session API does not expose a separate VoxCPM2 `set_instruct` call; LA Studio encodes the instruction in the text passed to `voxcpm2_synthesize`. Voice cloning uses a reference WAV through CrispASR's unified `crispasr_session_set_voice` API.

chunk_duration_sec.name: Chunk Duration
chunk_duration_sec.description: Target chunk duration in seconds for long-form generation. 0 disables chunking.

chunk_threshold_sec.name: Chunk Threshold
chunk_threshold_sec.description: Activate chunking above this estimated duration.

duration_sec.name: Duration
duration_sec.description: Force output duration in seconds. 0 uses runtime estimate.

preprocess_prompt.description: Clean and normalize the input text prompt.

voice_design_gender.description: Voice Design speaker gender. Auto leaves the prompt unchanged.

voice_design_age.description: Voice Design age keyword. Auto leaves the prompt unchanged.

voice_design_pitch.name: Pitch
voice_design_pitch.description: Voice Design pitch keyword. Auto leaves the prompt unchanged.

voice_design_style.name: Style
voice_design_style.description: Voice Design speaking style keyword. Auto leaves the prompt unchanged.

voice_design_english_accent.name: English Accent
voice_design_english_accent.description: English-only Voice Design accent keyword. Used only when the target language is English or Auto.

voice_design_chinese_dialect.name: Chinese Dialect
voice_design_chinese_dialect.description: Chinese-only Voice Design dialect keyword. Used only when the target language is Chinese or Auto.

readme.content:
# OmniVoice

OmniVoice is a massively multilingual zero-shot text-to-speech, voice cloning, and voice design model family developed by `k2-fsa` on Hugging Face.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/k2-fsa/OmniVoice
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/k2-fsa/sherpa-onnx

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Text-to-Speech, Zero-Shot Voice Cloning & Voice Design
- **Parameters:** ~1B
- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Architecture:** OmniVoice (GGUF)

## LA Studio Notes

OmniVoice in LA Studio is a high-fidelity speech synthesis workflow. It downloads GGUF models from conversion repository `Serveurperso/OmniVoice-GGUF`. It needs:
- Backbone: `omnivoice-base-Q8_0.gguf`
- Tokenizer: `omnivoice-tokenizer-F32.gguf`

It supports over 600 languages, zero-shot voice cloning, and description-based voice design. It also supports non-verbal tags like `[laughter]`, `[sigh]`, etc.

ttsFamilies.title: VibeVoice
ttsFamilies.subtitle: Realtime TTS with preset voice packs
ttsFamilies.description: Microsoft VibeVoice Realtime 0.5B packaged as CrispASR GGUF for low-latency local text-to-speech with selectable preset voice prompts.
strengths: Recommended Q4_K model is about 607 MB
Voice prompts are small GGUF packs
Runs through the shared CrispASR runtime
weaknesses: Realtime 0.5B uses preset voice packs, not dynamic WAV cloning
Fewer generation controls than larger VibeVoice variants

readme.content:
# VibeVoice

VibeVoice Realtime 0.5B is Microsoft's low-latency text-to-speech model. LA Studio installs the CrispASR GGUF build for local synthesis through the shared CrispASR runtime.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/microsoft/VibeVoice-Realtime-0.5B
- CrispASR GGUF artifacts: https://huggingface.co/cstr/vibevoice-realtime-0.5b-GGUF
- Runtime: https://github.com/CrispStrobe/CrispASR/releases/tag/v0.8.4

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Text-to-Speech
- **Parameters:** 500M (0.5B)
- **License:** MIT
- **Architecture:** VibeVoice Realtime GGUF
- **CrispASR backend:** `vibevoice-tts`

## LA Studio Notes

LA Studio downloads the recommended Q4_K talker and a required voice prompt GGUF from `cstr/vibevoice-realtime-0.5b-GGUF`:

- Talker: `vibevoice-realtime-0.5b-q4_k.gguf`
- Default voice: `vibevoice-voice-emma.gguf`
- Optional voices: `vibevoice-voice-en-Carter_man.gguf`, `vibevoice-voice-en-Emma_woman.gguf`, and other language voice packs

The realtime 0.5B model uses preset GGUF voice packs. It does not support dynamic WAV reference cloning in CrispASR; use a larger VibeVoice variant when runtime WAV cloning is required.

ttsFamilies.title: VoxCPM2
ttsFamilies.subtitle: 30-language TTS, voice design, and cloning
ttsFamilies.description: Tokenizer-free 2B multilingual TTS model with 48kHz output, natural-language voice design, and zero-shot voice cloning via reference WAV.
strengths: Natural-language voice design through the upstream parenthesized prompt format
weaknesses: VoxCPM2 support requires CrispASR v0.8.4 or newer
CrispASR does not expose a separate VoxCPM2 instruct API; voice design is encoded in the synthesis text

seed.name: Seed
seed.description: Random seed for reproducibility. -1 for random.

instruct.name: Voice Description
instruct.description: VoxCPM2 voice design description encoded in the upstream parenthesized prompt format.

VoxCPM2 is an open-weight multilingual text-to-speech model from OpenBMB. The upstream model card describes it as a tokenizer-free, diffusion-autoregressive 2B parameter TTS model with 48 kHz audio output, natural-language voice design, and reference-audio voice cloning.

LA Studio installs VoxCPM2 through the CrispASR GGUF conversion repository:

- Upstream model: https://huggingface.co/openbmb/VoxCPM2
- GGUF artifacts: https://huggingface.co/cstr/voxcpm2-GGUF
- Runtime: https://github.com/CrispStrobe/CrispASR/releases/tag/v0.8.4

## Supported Languages

Arabic, Burmese, Chinese, Danish, Dutch, English, Finnish, French, German, Greek, Hebrew, Hindi, Indonesian, Italian, Japanese, Khmer, Korean, Lao, Malay, Norwegian, Polish, Portuguese, Russian, Spanish, Swahili, Swedish, Tagalog, Thai, Turkish, Vietnamese.

## LA Studio Notes

VoxCPM2 requires a CrispASR runtime with the `voxcpm2-tts` backend. LA Studio uses CrispASR v0.8.4 or newer for this model.

Voice design is applied by prepending the voice description to the synthesis text in the upstream VoxCPM2 parenthesized prompt style, for example `(A warm, clear voice)Hello.`. CrispASR's current session API does not expose a separate VoxCPM2 `set_instruct` call; LA Studio encodes the instruction in the text passed to `voxcpm2_synthesize`. Voice cloning uses a reference WAV through CrispASR's unified `crispasr_session_set_voice` API.

readme.content:
# VieNeu-TTS v2 Turbo

VieNeu-TTS v2 Turbo is an advanced, lightweight bilingual Vietnamese-English text-to-speech and voice cloning model, published by `pnnbao-ump` on Hugging Face.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/pnnbao-ump/VieNeu-TTS-v2
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/dduongtrandai/speech-lm-tts.cpp

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Text-to-Speech & Voice Cloning
- **Parameters:** ~300M
- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Architecture:** VieNeu-TTS (GGUF)

## LA Studio Notes

VieNeu-TTS v2 Turbo in LA Studio is optimized for fast CPU execution and instant 3-5 seconds zero-shot voice cloning. It downloads files from conversion repositories:
- `pnnbao-ump/VieNeu-TTS-v2-Turbo-GGUF` for the GGUF backbone and preset voices configuration.
- `pnnbao-ump/VieNeu-Codec` for the speaker encoder and neural decoder ONNX models.

readme.content:
# VieNeu-TTS v3 Turbo

VieNeu-TTS v3 Turbo is a 48 kHz Vietnamese-English text-to-speech model from Pham Nguyen Ngoc Bao. LA Studio uses the native `VieNeu-TTS.cpp` runtime packages with the `vieneu-v3-native` pipeline for local synthesis and runtime-reported inference progress.

## LA Studio Notes

LA Studio targets `VieNeu-TTS.cpp` v0.1.1 with `pipelineProfile: vieneu-v3-native`. The runtime exposes a C ABI-compatible progress callback, so LA Studio can show exact inference progress while generating audio.

Required components are packaged in `lastudio-community/VieNeu-TTS-v3-Turbo-CPP` for LA Studio:

- VieNeu v3 config/tokenizer
- Native assets: `backbone.gguf`, `vieneu_v3_heads.npz`, and `acoustic/vieneu_acoustic_weights.npz`
- Preset voices: `voices_v3_turbo.json`
- MOSS Audio Tokenizer Nano ONNX codec files under `codec/`
- Runtime archives from https://github.com/dduongtrandai/VieNeu-TTS.cpp/releases

The native C++ runtime consumes local files only. It does not download model files at runtime.

readme.content:
# Qwen3-TTS Base 0.6B

Qwen3-TTS Base 0.6B is a lightweight zero-shot voice cloning text-to-speech model from the Alibaba Qwen team, published under the `Qwen` organization on Hugging Face.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-0.6B-Base
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen2.5-TTS

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Text-to-Speech (Zero-Shot Voice Cloning)
- **Parameters:** 600M (0.6B)
- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Architecture:** Qwen2.5-TTS GGUF

## LA Studio Notes

Qwen3-TTS Base 0.6B in LA Studio is a zero-shot voice cloning workflow. It downloads GGUF models from conversion repository `cstr/qwen3-tts-0.6b-base-GGUF`. It needs:
- Backbone: `qwen3-tts-12hz-0.6b-base-q4_k.gguf`, `qwen3-tts-12hz-0.6b-base-q8_0.gguf`, or `qwen3-tts-12hz-0.6b-base.gguf`
- Tokenizer: `qwen3-tts-tokenizer-12hz.gguf`

It requires a 24kHz reference WAV file and a reference transcript to clone a voice.

readme.content:
# Qwen3-TTS CustomVoice 1.7B

Qwen3-TTS CustomVoice 1.7B is a text-to-speech model from the Alibaba Qwen team specialized for preset speakers and style instructions, published under the `Qwen` organization on Hugging Face.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen2.5-TTS

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Text-to-Speech
- **Parameters:** 1.7B
- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Architecture:** Qwen2.5-TTS GGUF

## LA Studio Notes

Qwen3-TTS CustomVoice 1.7B in LA Studio is a preset speaker TTS workflow. It downloads GGUF models from conversion repository `cstr/qwen3-tts-1.7b-customvoice-GGUF`. It needs:
- Backbone: `qwen3-tts-12hz-1.7b-customvoice-q8_0.gguf`
- Tokenizer: `qwen3-tts-tokenizer-12hz.gguf`

It comes with 9 default preset speakers: aiden, dylan, eric, ono_anna, ryan, serena, sohee, uncle_fu, and vivian. You can also supply text instructions to control the style of the generated speech.

readme.content:
# Qwen3-TTS 1.7B VoiceDesign

Qwen3-TTS 1.7B VoiceDesign is a text-to-speech model from the Alibaba Qwen team specialized in zero-shot voice design from text instructions, published under the `Qwen` organization on Hugging Face.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-VoiceDesign
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen2.5-TTS

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Text-to-Speech (Voice Design)
- **Parameters:** 1.7B
- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Architecture:** Qwen2.5-TTS GGUF

## LA Studio Notes

Qwen3-TTS 1.7B VoiceDesign in LA Studio is a voice design workflow. It downloads GGUF models from conversion repository `cstr/qwen3-tts-1.7b-voicedesign-GGUF`. It needs:
- Backbone: `qwen3-tts-12hz-1.7b-voicedesign-q8_0.gguf`
- Tokenizer: `qwen3-tts-tokenizer-12hz.gguf`

It allows designing unique synthetic voices on the fly by specifying a text style instruction/description alongside the text prompt to synthesize.

readme.content:
# Nemotron-3.5 ASR Streaming 0.6B

Nemotron-3.5 ASR Streaming 0.6B is NVIDIA's multilingual, cache-aware streaming automatic-speech-recognition model. It uses a FastConformer encoder and RNN-T decoder.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/nvidia/nemotron-3.5-asr-streaming-0.6b
- CrispASR backend: https://github.com/CrispStrobe/CrispASR/releases/tag/v0.8.4

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Speech-to-Text
- **Parameters:** 0.6B
- **License:** OpenMDW-1.1
- **Languages:** 35 languages, including Vietnamese
- **Architecture:** Cache-aware FastConformer RNN-T

## LA Studio Notes

LA Studio runs this model locally with the `nemotron` backend in CrispASR v0.8.4 or later. The app downloads GGUF artifacts from `cstr/nemotron-3.5-asr-streaming-0.6b-GGUF`:

- `nemotron-3.5-asr-streaming-0.6b-q4_k.gguf` (recommended, 479 MB)
- `nemotron-3.5-asr-streaming-0.6b-f16.gguf` (1.3 GB)

The model is streaming-native. LA Studio's current STT workflow transcribes selected or recorded audio as a completed job; live incremental transcript delivery requires a future CrispASR session-stream API for Nemotron.

sttFamilies.title: Whisper.cpp
sttFamilies.subtitle: Offline multilingual speech recognition and translation
sttFamilies.description: Native C/C++ inference for OpenAI Whisper models converted to the custom ggml format. Supports multilingual transcription, automatic language detection and translation to English, with optional timestamp, TinyDiarize and Silero VAD workflows in upstream whisper.cpp.
strengths: Runs speech recognition locally with CPU and GPU runtime options
Supports 100 languages, automatic language detection and translation to English
Upstream supports token timestamps, TinyDiarize speaker-turn detection and Silero VAD
weaknesses: Large models require substantial download size and memory
TinyDiarize requires a dedicated model ending in -tdrz
LA Studio currently exposes only language, threads and translation controls from the upstream setting set

threads.name: Threads
threads.description: Number of computation threads. Upstream defaults to the smaller of 4 and the available hardware thread count.

translate.name: Translate to English
translate.description: Translate recognized speech into English instead of transcribing in its original language.

language.name: Language
language.description: Language code used for transcription. Upstream defaults to English; LA Studio accepts auto to enable automatic detection.

detect_language.name: Detect Language
detect_language.description: Automatically identify the spoken language before decoding.

offset_ms.name: Offset (ms)
offset_ms.description: Start processing at this audio offset.

duration_ms.name: Duration (ms)
duration_ms.description: Limit processing duration; 0 processes all remaining audio.

n_max_text_ctx.name: Maximum Text Context
n_max_text_ctx.description: Maximum number of previous text tokens available as decoder context.

no_context.name: Disable Previous Text Context
no_context.description: Do not feed previously decoded text as prompt to the next audio segment.

single_segment.name: Single Segment
single_segment.description: Force the decoded result into one segment.

print_special.name: Print Special Tokens
print_special.description: Include special tokens in printed output.

print_progress.name: Print Progress
print_progress.description: Print inference progress updates when using upstream output callbacks or examples.

thold_pt.name: Token Timestamp Probability Threshold
thold_pt.description: Probability threshold for token timestamp processing.

thold_ptsum.name: Token Timestamp Sum Threshold
thold_ptsum.description: Probability-sum threshold for token timestamp processing.

max_len.name: Maximum Segment Length
max_len.description: Maximum segment length in characters; 0 leaves segments unconstrained.

split_on_word.name: Split On Word
split_on_word.description: When segment length is constrained, split only on word boundaries.

max_tokens.name: Maximum Tokens Per Segment
max_tokens.description: Maximum tokens in a segment; 0 disables this constraint.

audio_ctx.name: Audio Context
audio_ctx.description: Override the model audio context length; 0 uses the model default.

tdrz_enable.name: TinyDiarize Speaker Turns
tdrz_enable.description: Enable speaker-turn detection. Requires a dedicated TinyDiarize model such as ggml-small.en-tdrz.bin.

initial_prompt.name: Initial Prompt
initial_prompt.description: Text prompt used to guide initial transcription context.

carry_initial_prompt.name: Carry Initial Prompt
carry_initial_prompt.description: Apply the initial prompt to each internal decoding call.

suppress_blank.name: Suppress Blank Tokens
suppress_blank.description: Suppress blank output tokens during decoding.

suppress_regex.name: Suppress Regex
suppress_regex.description: Regular expression for suppressing matching tokens from the output.

length_penalty.name: Length Penalty
length_penalty.description: Decoder length penalty; negative values keep the default behavior.

temperature_inc.name: Temperature Increment
temperature_inc.description: Temperature fallback increment when decoding fails quality thresholds.

entropy_thold.name: Entropy Threshold
entropy_thold.description: Entropy threshold used for decoding fallback.

logprob_thold.name: Log Probability Threshold
logprob_thold.description: Average log probability threshold used for decoding fallback.

no_speech_thold.name: No Speech Threshold
no_speech_thold.description: Probability threshold for identifying no-speech segments.

best_of.name: Greedy Best Of
best_of.description: Number of candidates considered by greedy decoding.

beam_size.name: Beam Size
beam_size.description: Number of beams used when the beam-search strategy is selected.

beam_patience.name: Beam Search Patience
beam_patience.description: Beam-search patience value; negative values retain upstream default behavior.

grammar.name: Grammar
grammar.description: GBNF grammar text used to constrain decoder output.

grammar_rule.name: Grammar Start Rule
grammar_rule.description: Root rule name used when grammar constraints are supplied.

grammar_penalty.name: Grammar Penalty
grammar_penalty.description: Penalty applied to tokens rejected by the grammar.

vad.name: Voice Activity Detection
vad.description: Enable Silero VAD-assisted filtering. Requires an optional VAD model such as ggml-silero-v6.2.0.bin.

vad_model_path.name: VAD Model Path
vad_model_path.description: Filesystem path of the optional Silero VAD model, for example ggml-silero-v6.2.0.bin.

vad_threshold.name: VAD Threshold
vad_threshold.description: Speech probability threshold used by Silero VAD.

vad_min_speech_duration_ms.name: VAD Minimum Speech Duration (ms)
vad_min_speech_duration_ms.description: Minimum duration for a detected speech region.

vad_min_silence_duration_ms.name: VAD Minimum Silence Duration (ms)
vad_min_silence_duration_ms.description: Minimum silence duration required to split speech regions.

vad_max_speech_duration_s.name: VAD Maximum Speech Duration (s)
vad_max_speech_duration_s.description: Maximum speech segment duration. Upstream defaults to FLT_MAX, effectively disabling forced splitting.

vad_speech_pad_ms.name: VAD Speech Padding (ms)
vad_speech_pad_ms.description: Padding added before and after detected speech segments.

vad_samples_overlap.name: VAD Samples Overlap (s)
vad_samples_overlap.description: Audio overlap in seconds used while applying VAD-filtered transcription.

use_gpu.name: Use GPU
use_gpu.description: Use GPU acceleration for the transcription model when the selected runtime supports it. Upstream currently forces VAD inference to CPU.

gpu_device.name: GPU Device
gpu_device.description: GPU device index used for model execution.

flash_attn.name: Flash Attention
flash_attn.description: Enable flash attention in the model context. Upstream disables DTW token timestamps while flash attention is active.

dtw_token_timestamps.name: DTW Token Timestamps
dtw_token_timestamps.description: Enable DTW alignment-head token timestamps. This requires flash attention to be disabled.

dtw_n_top.name: DTW Top Text Layers
dtw_n_top.description: Number of top text layers used by the DTW N-top-most alignment-head mode.

dtw_mem_size.name: DTW Memory Size (bytes)
dtw_mem_size.description: Maximum memory allocated for DTW timestamp processing.

dtw_preset.name: DTW Alignment Preset
dtw_preset.description: Model-specific alignment-head preset used for DTW token timestamps.

requiredFiles.purpose: Core transcription model in whisper.cpp custom ggml format

readme.content:
# Whisper

Whisper is a state-of-the-art automatic speech recognition (ASR) and speech translation model family developed by OpenAI, published under the `openai` organization on Hugging Face.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/openai/whisper-large-v3
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/openai/whisper

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Speech-to-Text (ASR) & Speech Translation
- **License:** MIT
- **Architecture:** Whisper (GGML)

## LA Studio Notes

Whisper.cpp in LA Studio runs inference locally using CPU and GPU options. It downloads GGML-converted models from conversion repository `ggerganov/whisper.cpp`. It needs:
- Backbone: `ggml-base.bin` (or other supported GGML files).

It supports 100 languages, automatic language detection, translation to English, token timestamps, and speaker diarization workflows.

readme.content:
# Qwen3-ASR 0.6B

Qwen3-ASR 0.6B is a lightweight speech-to-text and audio Q&A model from the Alibaba Qwen team, published under the `Qwen` organization on Hugging Face.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-ASR-0.6B
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen2.5-ASR

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Speech-to-Text (Transcription and Audio Q&A)
- **Parameters:** 600M (0.6B)
- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Architecture:** Qwen3-ASR GGUF

## LA Studio Notes

Qwen3-ASR 0.6B in LA Studio runs offline using the CrispASR runtime. It downloads GGUF models from conversion repository `cstr/qwen3-asr-0.6b-GGUF`. It needs:
- Model: `qwen3-asr-0.6b-q4_k.gguf`

It supports standard multilingual speech transcription as well as free-form audio Q&A instructions via the `Audio Q&A Prompt` configuration setting.

readme.content:
# Qwen3-ASR 1.7B

Qwen3-ASR 1.7B is a medium speech-to-text and audio Q&A model from the Alibaba Qwen team, published under the `Qwen` organization on Hugging Face.

## Links

- Upstream model card: https://huggingface.co/Qwen/Qwen3-ASR-1.7B
- Upstream GitHub: https://github.com/QwenLM/Qwen2.5-ASR

## Model Facts

- **Task:** Speech-to-Text (Transcription and Audio Q&A)
- **Parameters:** 1.7B
- **License:** Apache-2.0
- **Architecture:** Qwen3-ASR GGUF

## LA Studio Notes

Qwen3-ASR 1.7B in LA Studio runs offline using the CrispASR runtime. It downloads GGUF models from conversion repository `cstr/qwen3-asr-1.7b-GGUF`. It needs one of the following model files:
- `qwen3-asr-1.7b-q4_k.gguf` (Recommended, 1.3 GB)
- `qwen3-asr-1.7b-q8_0.gguf` (2.5 GB)
- `qwen3-asr-1.7b-f16.gguf` (4.7 GB)

It supports standard multilingual speech transcription as well as free-form audio Q&A instructions via the `Audio Q&A Prompt` configuration setting.
